segments.text: Ladies and gentlemen, please welcome to the stage Blue Diamond leader Elena Dwan-Daisi.
Do you know what the most frustrating feeling is when you want to have?
The most difficult is to have the heart but not the means.
I've only felt blessed like life has given me everything I needed, but at the same time I felt the need to give back.
And no matter what job I had, I always wanted to be involved in projects to help people.
But for a long time I couldn't actually do it.
I worked in architecture. I designed homes for children in Morocco when I was there.
A center for people with Alzheimer in Marseille. But the projects stayed on paper. They never came to life.
And over time, frustration started to grow inside me. I wanted to help, but I didn't have the tools.
Have you ever felt that you want to help, but you don't know how?
For me, everything started in 2016 when I discovered doTERRA. And I began helping people with the all that I had.
But two years later, I discovered something that changed everything.
I was a friend of mine. The OTERRA, Hidden and Foundation. I kept seeing it everywhere on stage, on OTERRA events.
Like real projects. Really impact. And I kept asking myself the same question. Can I do this? Is it hard?
I had every possible doubt. But I also had a wish. A simple wish from my heart.
And in December 2020 to 2019, together with my COTERRA team, we collected gifts for Christmas, for children's villages.
It was beautiful. Fourteen houses were filled with our gifts. But at the same time, I felt I wanted to do more.
Because a gift, you know, passes. But a hope, a hope means support every single day.
And I started myself. Could I do more for them? They needed help. It wasn't just an idea. It was a real need.

And for the first time, I felt that I could make a difference. If this project could be part of Healing Hands Foundation, it could support a home for an entire year. A full year of everything they needed.
And that's when I asked Emily. In February 2020, I had the opportunity to meet Emily Wright.
And I went up to her. I was a little bit anxious in that moment. And I told her about my project. And I asked her,
Do you think it's eligible? Do you think I can apply this project to Healing Hands?
And she looked at me. She smiled like Emily Blue. She took my hands like this. And said, Yes! Go for it!
And you know, sometimes, a simple yes can change everything. And then I had, yeah, Emily deserved it.
And then I had assumed someone that I had been following. It was someone who had already done Healing Hands project.
Her name is Rebecca Valls. She wasn't part of my team. But I immediately felt her energy.
And after I found somehow the courage to ask her how to apply, she showed me. I mean, we did it together. Step by step.
And this one, I realized something. It's not complicated. It's not hard. It's straightforward.
I went on the platform and applied. I think the process itself took me about 10 to 20 minutes. So you just go on the platform, you know,
with the Healing Hands.org. And you see if your product qualifies, if you fit in all the details. And that's it.
And just like that, it's frustration. Just become real for me. So if you are wondering how does a project look like, something like that. Just one page.
And after that moment, it's a little bit more. And after that moment, it was the first step on changing someone's life. That's all, guys.
And after my first project, I realized something. These projects don't just change the life of the people you have. They also change you.
And with every project. And with every project, I proved. One, then two, then four, five projects. And for the first time, they were not just projects on paper. They came to life.

They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They didn't go. They
And then, in 2021, I saw an announcement from the Don'ty Association.
They were raising funds for children who needed to travel abroad.
For heart surgeries that couldn't be done in Romania.
And I felt that I had to do something.
I had to speak with Adelina, the founder.
Someone I had been always seen on TV and online.
I remember I was thinking, what if she won't take me seriously?
We were no longer talking about a house.
About new ones fighting to survive.
And I was there in that plane.
And I saw how fragile life really is.
Doctors keeping those children alive during the flight.
Because I had to face something I had been avoiding for a long time.
I had to speak with people from all kinds of backgrounds.
People who had the power to support this project.
And my voice was shaking also.
I approached the well-known chef in Romania.
Also someone I had almost seen on TV.
And I almost didn't dare to talk to him.
My voice was like a man full of emotion.
And my mind was full of scenarios.
And when a need is bigger than you.
Fear has no place to stay.
And we could give those children.
And I was no longer speaking with fear.
I was no longer seeing rejection.
It's easy for me to say.
I had a project for children.
I want to be part of this.
She didn't just wanted to help.
She wanted to grow with me.
And she trusted me to guide her.
And she became a great leader.
And she is still with us.
Like a leg for Blue Diamond.
And this is not just a single story.
Was never just about the audience.
When hearts come together for good.
We are not just building projects.
I can say with all my heart.
If you put in the work.
And they were made by people.
You don't have to look for the project. They find you when you choose to see. Because there is a lot of suffering around us.
You know, there is also so much good waiting to be done. And maybe, maybe we can't help everyone. But we can help someone.
And when we do that together, we change everything. So, I want to go to leave you, not to leave you with a thought.
Don't wait for the perfect moment. Don't wait until you have it all figured out. Just choose to be open. And look around you. Open. Keep your eyes open. And your heart even more open.
Be present. Because people have names. And maybe, they didn't feel like this before. They should have the heart for nothing.
Today, you have both. And maybe, you are their answer. You can be their answer.
